title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 347.175 - Derivative action — attorney's fees
text:
347.175.  Derivative action — attorney's fees. — If a derivative action is successful, in whole or in part, or if anything is received by the plaintiff as a result of a judgment, compromise or settlement of an action or claim, the court may award the plaintiff reasonable expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees, and shall direct him to remit to the limited liability company the remainder of those proceeds received by him.
(L. 1993 S.B. 66 & 20 § 359.820) 
Effective 12-01-93
